raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("failure rates of dental grafts in different classes of gum recession")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed evidence reviewed:
1. Severe gingival recession (Classes III & IV) indeed poses challenges for graft survival given the compromised structural foundation present.
2. Studies indicate variable efficacy among different types of grafting procedures according to lesion severity and anatomy involved—factors strongly influenced by recession level.
Given these points, my revised stance remains aligned with initial conclusions emphasizing difficulty faced by complex cases requiring more invasive measures:
\boxed{D} Class IV Recession continues presenting greatest challenge aligning well-supported findings correlating increased complexity with heightened failure probabilities despite general suitability of certain techniques otherwise applied effectively elsewhere along continuum defining clinical practice today. Thus, maintaining selection preference focuses appropriately highlighting key distinctions noted between varying degrees of involvement shaping overall prognosis expectations realistically set forth clearly articulated herein.
